Summary: “Eraserhead!” the high pitched voice of one of the many security robots that traversed the school, called over the speaker in his room. “Student on the roof of the dorm! Its passed curfew! Deal with it!” The alarm ended, thankfully, once the robot made its announcement. Sighing and rubbing the back of his neck in agitation, Shouta reached for his phone to check the time. Clicking his phone on, the time revealed it to be 2:00 in the morning! Obviously he wasn’t the only one who should have been asleep in bed. Grabbing his capture weapon from the hook on his door, he quickly left his apartment and made his way to the roof. *I swear if I find Tokoyami and Dark Shadow screaming into the void, I will throw them into the void!* Shouta thought to himself as he pushed open the roof top door. It took a moment for his eyes to adjust to the darkness outside, and when they did he felt his blood run cold with fear. Sitting on the edge of the roof looking down was none other than his number one problem child, Midoriya Izuku. What alarmed him even more was the soft sniffles and whimpering sounds coming from the boy.

Summary: In order to obtain something, something else of equal value must be lost. Midoriya Izuku should have been a hero. He had the heart, the determination, and a powerful healing quirk to boot. The funny thing about quirks is they aren't magic. Everything has a cost, in the case of Recovery Girl, she uses up her patients stamina to heal them. Izuku did not have this drawback. He had a far worse one. In order to heal, he replaces his patients health, to obtain the healing energy to do so, he must harm others. By taking away the health of others, he can give it to another. To obtain something, something else of equal value must be lost. Ridiculed by his peers for being a villain that only harms and takes from others, Izuku starts to shrink away from his dream. He could still try, but what good is a hero that harms people. One night Izuku stumbles upon a hero bleeding out in an alleyway. He saves the mans life, and vows that if he cannot be a hero, he can at least save them. Becoming a vigilante, Izuku is the hero for heroes. Reaching out in their darkest moments, and coming to their aid.

Summary: “I’m your editor,” she says. “And I’m not editing the crap you wrote while half delirious on --” she picks up one of his cans -- “Calpis Yogurt Flavored Milk Soda -- Midoriya, how do you even have any brain cells left?” “The only thing that keeps me kicking is katsudon and spite.” Suzuki holds up both hands. “How many fingers am I holding up?” “Ten,” he answers immediately, albeit smugly. Summary: Eraserhead went on a raid that night expecting the worst. Kids were reported missing. Quirkless kids. A case that went on for years was finally coming to fruition. A warehouse. An unsuspecting building which, from their intel and scouting, could house the missing children and the criminals responsible for this crime. Eraserhead has expected the worst. Starving children. Injured children. Even dead children. What he wasn't expecting was only one child. A hurt child. A child with a very powerful quirk. --- Izuku only knew pain for most of his life until someone saved him. All he wanted was to be a hero. He has the quirk to achieve his dream. His dad told him he can be a hero. But as powerful as his quirk is, there's a reason he has such a powerful quirk. He has an artificial quirk. And it wasn't his own.

Summary: “Ask me your question.” Viridian jolted at the sudden demand, bright green eyes peeking over Shouta’s arm to stare up at the older man, his face twisted in confusion and just a hint of fear. “W-what?” “When I got here,” Shouta clarified. “You wanted to ask me a question. Ask it now.” The vigilante frowned, breath hitching again in his throat as he stared. “C-can I b-be a hero even though- even though I’m quirkless?” And Shouta reached one hand up, brushed the curls and the tears away from the boy’s face and gave him the gentlest smile he could before saying a soft, honest, “yes. Yes  you can.” Or The hundredth fanfic you've seen where Aizawa adopts the adorable vigilante named Izuku Midoriya. Because I needed to write one to satisfy my brain. Please enjoy.

Summary: “Eraserhead!” the high pitched voice of one of the many security robots that traversed the school, called over the speaker in his room. “Student on the roof of the dorm! Its passed curfew! Deal with it!” The alarm ended, thankfully, once the robot made its announcement. Sighing and rubbing the back of his neck in agitation, Shouta reached for his phone to check the time. Clicking his phone on, the time revealed it to be 2:00 in the morning! Obviously he wasn’t the only one who should have been asleep in bed. Grabbing his capture weapon from the hook on his door, he quickly left his apartment and made his way to the roof. *I swear if I find Tokoyami and Dark Shadow screaming into the void, I will throw them into the void!* Shouta thought to himself as he pushed open the roof top door. It took a moment for his eyes to adjust to the darkness outside, and when they did he felt his blood run cold with fear. Sitting on the edge of the roof looking down was none other than his number one problem child, Midoriya Izuku. What alarmed him even more was the soft sniffles and whimpering sounds coming from the boy.

Summary: Izuku Midoriya was a quirkless, plain-looking, weak, orphan, with a bad reputation. The boy was used to being humiliated, to being hated, to being pushed around. He was used to being alone, to being left behind, to being broken down... And most of all, he was used to being cursed with the absence of a quirk, in a society where quirks were everything... He had imagined that things would be different, when he was with the people that shared his goals... Because they were supposed to be heroes-in-training, he had expected they would learn to protect each other and be able to help each other do good... When he arrived at UA, things hadn't changed that much... but at least in some ways they had. Now he had to deal with the principal and his homeroom teacher, that were watching him like a hawk, looking for the first opportunity to expel him. Along with a couple of classmates (schoolmates), who seemed to target him in almost every instance, and a certain white-haired villain and his group that couldn't seem to leave him alone. or Izuku, winning the hearts of literally everyone without even trying (or wanting to). {not a dekubowl fic}

Summary: In order to obtain something, something else of equal value must be lost. Midoriya Izuku should have been a hero. He had the heart, the determination, and a powerful healing quirk to boot. The funny thing about quirks is they aren't magic. Everything has a cost, in the case of Recovery Girl, she uses up her patients stamina to heal them. Izuku did not have this drawback. He had a far worse one. In order to heal, he replaces his patients health, to obtain the healing energy to do so, he must harm others. By taking away the health of others, he can give it to another. To obtain something, something else of equal value must be lost. Ridiculed by his peers for being a villain that only harms and takes from others, Izuku starts to shrink away from his dream. He could still try, but what good is a hero that harms people. One night Izuku stumbles upon a hero bleeding out in an alleyway. He saves the mans life, and vows that if he cannot be a hero, he can at least save them. Becoming a vigilante, Izuku is the hero for heroes. Reaching out in their darkest moments, and coming to their aid.

Summary: “I’m your editor,” she says. “And I’m not editing the crap you wrote while half delirious on --” she picks up one of his cans -- “Calpis Yogurt Flavored Milk Soda -- Midoriya, how do you even have any brain cells left?” “The only thing that keeps me kicking is katsudon and spite.” Suzuki holds up both hands. “How many fingers am I holding up?” “Ten,” he answers immediately, albeit smugly. Summary: Eraserhead went on a raid that night expecting the worst. Kids were reported missing. Quirkless kids. A case that went on for years was finally coming to fruition. A warehouse. An unsuspecting building which, from their intel and scouting, could house the missing children and the criminals responsible for this crime. Eraserhead has expected the worst. Starving children. Injured children. Even dead children. What he wasn't expecting was only one child. A hurt child. A child with a very powerful quirk. --- Izuku only knew pain for most of his life until someone saved him. All he wanted was to be a hero. He has the quirk to achieve his dream. His dad told him he can be a hero. But as powerful as his quirk is, there's a reason he has such a powerful quirk. He has an artificial quirk. And it wasn't his own.

Summary: “Ask me your question.” Viridian jolted at the sudden demand, bright green eyes peeking over Shouta’s arm to stare up at the older man, his face twisted in confusion and just a hint of fear. “W-what?” “When I got here,” Shouta clarified. “You wanted to ask me a question. Ask it now.” The vigilante frowned, breath hitching again in his throat as he stared. “C-can I b-be a hero even though- even though I’m quirkless?” And Shouta reached one hand up, brushed the curls and the tears away from the boy’s face and gave him the gentlest smile he could before saying a soft, honest, “yes. Yes  you can.” Or The hundredth fanfic you've seen where Aizawa adopts the adorable vigilante named Izuku Midoriya. Because I needed to write one to satisfy my brain. Please enjoy.

Summary: Opening his eyes fully, he saw the area he was placed on. There were no traditional beds in the room, instead there was one big nest in the center of the room - which he was laying on now. They were some of the softest blankets he had ever felt. Slowly running a hand over one of the blankets, he gripped it and pulled it over himself, trying to maintain any sense of modesty he may still have. It was a pretty purple blanket, he kinda liked it. A grunting noise threw him out of his sense of false calm as he quickly gripped the blanket up over his chest as snapped his head up, meeting not one, not two, but four sets of eyes all staring at him interested and… was that confusion? Maybe even a little concern? Wait, was this their room, their den?

Summary: When a pregnant, newly divorced Inko adopts her nephew Tenko after he'd accidentally killed his entire family in a quirk accident, she has no idea what to do or how she's supposed to be able to take care of all three of them at the same time. But, that's what friends are for, right? Mitsuki and Masaru suggest that she live with them until she has her life back under control. Fifteen years later, they were still living together. Maybe they weren't the most typical family, but they loved each other, and that's all that mattered, right? I was up late at night, and then this idea somehow came into existence. I just wanted a sweet fic about Izuku and Tenko being siblings and then the Bakugous came into the picture somehow? I really don't have a good explanation for it, to be honest.
